appeal_letter:
Dear UnitedHealthcare Appeals Reviewer,

I am appealing the denial of prior authorization for my transfer from acute hospital care to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (IRF), which instead authorized a transfer to a Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF).

The denial states that, based on UnitedHealthcare guidelines, admission to an IRF requires evidence that the patient can actively participate in and benefit from at least three hours of intensive therapy per day. The denial cited hospital physical therapy notes indicating that I fatigued after 30 minutes of therapy.

The basis for this appeal is that the criterion the denial relied on, applied to my current documented condition, supports IRF admission rather than an SNF. The single note showing fatigue after 30 minutes reflects therapy performed on the day immediately following my stroke. It does not represent my current ability to participate in and benefit from therapy. Since that day, I have been tolerating two hours of therapy per day. The older note appears to have been submitted to the review without the more recent therapy records that document this progress.

I had a severe stroke and am working to regain my ability to walk. An SNF would not provide the intensive daily therapy that my recovery requires, whereas my demonstrated and improving therapy tolerance is consistent with the participation and benefit that the IRF criterion describes. Because my current tolerance was not reflected in the records reviewed, I ask that the decision be reconsidered on the basis of my up-to-date therapy participation. To the extent the three-hour threshold is read strictly, I ask that an individualized assessment of my present condition and rehabilitation potential be considered.

The denial letter did not state my appeal rights or any deadline for requesting an appeal or further review. I request that these be provided.

I request that the denial be overturned and that prior authorization for transfer to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility be approved based on my current therapy participation, and that my updated hospital therapy notes be obtained and reviewed.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Current hospital physical and occupational therapy notes documenting the recent two-hours-per-day therapy tolerance
Dated comparison showing the 30-minute fatigue note was from the day immediately after the stroke versus subsequent progress notes
A physician or rehabilitation specialist statement on rehabilitation potential and ability to participate in and benefit from intensive IRF therapy
Documentation of the severe stroke diagnosis and current functional status (including walking/mobility goals)
The specific UnitedHealthcare IRF medical-necessity guideline text relied upon in the denial
The plan's appeal rights, deadlines, and instructions for internal appeal or external review
